name: Marie Curie
tagline: Two-Time Nobel Laureate Who Discovered Radioactivity
taglineCn: 发现放射性的两次诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Marie Skłodowska Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize, the first person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences, and remains the only person to achieve this distinction. Born in Warsaw, she moved to Paris where she and her husband Pierre discovered polonium and radium. Her research on radioactivity — a term she coined — fundamentally changed physics and chemistry and led to developments in cancer treatment. She died from aplastic anemia, likely caused by prolonged exposure to radiation.
bioCn: 玛丽·斯克沃多夫斯卡·居里是第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女性，也是第一位在两个不同科学领域获得诺贝尔奖的人，至今仍是唯一做到这一点的人。她出生于华沙，搬到巴黎后与丈夫皮埃尔一起发现了钋和镭。她对放射性——这个词正是她创造的——的研究从根本上改变了物理学和化学，并推动了癌症治疗的发展。她因再生障碍性贫血去世，这很可能是长期暴露于辐射造成的。
achievements: First woman to win a Nobel Prize (Physics, 1903)
Only person to win Nobel Prizes in two sciences (Chemistry, 1911)
Discovered polonium and radium
Coined the term 'radioactivity'
achievementsCn: 第一位获得诺贝尔奖的女性（物理学，1903年）
唯一在两个科学领域获诺贝尔奖的人（化学，1911年）
发现钋和镭
创造了'放射性'一词
quote: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood. Now is the time to understand more, so that we may fear less.
quoteCn: 生活中没有什么是值得恐惧的，只有需要理解的。现在是更多理解的时候，以便我们可以少些恐惧。
references: Quinn, Susan. Marie Curie: A Life. Simon & Schuster, 1995.

name: Lise Meitner
tagline: The Physicist Who Explained Nuclear Fission
taglineCn: 解释核裂变的物理学家
bio: Lise Meitner was an Austrian-Swedish physicist who provided the first theoretical explanation of nuclear fission. After fleeing Nazi Germany in 1938, she correctly interpreted the results of experiments by her longtime collaborator Otto Hahn, recognizing that the uranium nucleus had been split. Despite being nominated for the Nobel Prize 49 times, she was excluded from the 1944 Chemistry Nobel — a decision Einstein called 'unjust.' Element 109, meitnerium, is named in her honor.
bioCn: 丽泽·迈特纳是一位奥地利裔瑞典物理学家，她提供了核裂变的第一个理论解释。1938年逃离纳粹德国后，她正确解读了长期合作者奥托·哈恩的实验结果，认识到铀核已被分裂。尽管被诺贝尔奖提名49次，她却被排除在1944年的诺贝尔化学奖之外——爱因斯坦称这一决定'不公正'。第109号元素鿏以她的名字命名。
achievements: Provided first theoretical explanation of nuclear fission
Nominated for Nobel Prize 49 times
Element 109 (meitnerium) named in her honor
achievementsCn: 提供了核裂变的第一个理论解释
被诺贝尔奖提名49次
第109号元素以她的名字命名
quote: Science makes people reach selflessly for truth and objectivity; it teaches people to accept reality, with wonder and admiration.
quoteCn: 科学使人无私地追求真理和客观性；它教人以惊奇和赞叹接受现实。
references: Sime, Ruth Lewin. Lise Meitner: A Life in Physics. Univ of California Press, 1996.

name: Chien-Shiung Wu
bio: Chien-Shiung Wu conducted the famous Wu experiment in 1957, which proved that parity is not conserved in weak nuclear interactions — overturning a fundamental assumption of physics. While her male colleagues Tsung-Dao Lee and Chen-Ning Yang received the Nobel Prize for the theoretical prediction, Wu's experimental proof was excluded from the award. Known as the 'First Lady of Physics' and the 'Chinese Marie Curie,' she received the inaugural Wolf Prize in Physics and was honored with a U.S. postage stamp.
bioCn: 吴健雄在1957年进行了著名的吴氏实验，证明了在弱核相互作用中宇称不守恒——推翻了物理学的一个基本假设。虽然她的男性同事李政道和杨振宁因理论预测获得了诺贝尔奖，但吴健雄的实验证明却被排除在奖项之外。她被称为'物理学第一夫人'和'中国的居里夫人'，获得了首届沃尔夫物理学奖，并被印在美国邮票上。
achievements: Proved parity non-conservation (Wu experiment)
Received inaugural Wolf Prize in Physics
Honored with U.S. postage stamp (2021)
achievementsCn: 证明宇称不守恒（吴氏实验）
获得首届沃尔夫物理学奖
被印在美国邮票上（2021年）
quote: There is only one thing worse than coming home from the lab to a sink full of dirty dishes. And that is not going to the lab at all!
quoteCn: 唯一比从实验室回家面对一水池脏碗碟更糟的事，就是根本没去实验室！
references: Chiang, Tsai-Chien. Madame Wu Chien-Shiung: The First Lady of Physics Research. World Scientific, 2014.

name: Maria Goeppert Mayer
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Nuclear Shell Model
taglineCn: 核壳层模型诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Maria Goeppert Mayer was a theoretical physicist who proposed the nuclear shell model of the atomic nucleus, for which she received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1963 — only the second woman to do so after Marie Curie. For much of her career, anti-nepotism rules prevented her from holding paid positions at universities where her husband worked, forcing her to pursue research as a volunteer or in unpaid positions.
bioCn: 玛丽亚·格佩特-梅耶是一位理论物理学家，她提出了原子核的核壳层模型，并因此获得1963年诺贝尔物理学奖——继居里夫人之后第二位获此殊荣的女性。在她职业生涯的大部分时间里，反裙带关系规定阻止她在丈夫工作的大学担任有薪职位，迫使她以志愿者或无薪职位进行研究。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (1963) for nuclear shell model
Second woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Pioneered two-photon absorption theory
achievementsCn: 因核壳层模型获诺贝尔物理学奖（1963年）
第二位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
开创两光子吸收理论

name: Émilie du Châtelet
taglineCn: 将牛顿《原理》翻译成法语
bio: Émilie du Châtelet was a French natural philosopher and mathematician who made Newton's work accessible to the European continent. Her translation and commentary on Newton's Principia Mathematica, published posthumously, remains the standard French translation to this day. She also proposed that kinetic energy is proportional to the square of velocity — a concept later confirmed and foundational to modern physics. She died at 42 from complications of childbirth, days after completing her translation.
bioCn: 埃米莉·杜·夏特莱是一位法国自然哲学家和数学家，她使牛顿的著作在欧洲大陆得到传播。她对牛顿《自然哲学的数学原理》的翻译和评注在她去世后出版，至今仍是标准的法语译本。她还提出动能与速度的平方成正比——这一概念后来得到证实，成为现代物理学的基础。她在完成翻译后数天，因分娩并发症去世，年仅42岁。
achievements: Translated Newton's Principia Mathematica into French
Proposed kinetic energy proportional to velocity squared
Her translation remains the French standard after 275 years
achievementsCn: 将牛顿《自然哲学的数学原理》翻译成法语
提出动能与速度平方成正比
她的译本在275年后仍是法语标准版
references: Zinsser, Judith. Emilie Du Chatelet: Daring Genius of the Enlightenment. Penguin, 2007.

name: Donna Strickland
taglineCn: 激光物理诺贝尔奖得主
bio: Donna Strickland shared the 2018 Nobel Prize in Physics for developing chirped pulse amplification (CPA), a technique that generates ultrashort, high-intensity laser pulses now used in millions of corrective eye surgeries annually. She was only the third woman in history to receive the Nobel Prize in Physics. At the time of her award, she was an associate professor — she did not yet have a Wikipedia page, highlighting the persistent invisibility of women in science.
bioCn: 唐娜·斯特里克兰因开发啁啾脉冲放大（CPA）技术而分享了2018年诺贝尔物理学奖，该技术产生超短、高强度激光脉冲，现在每年用于数百万次矫正眼科手术。她是历史上第三位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性。获奖时她还是副教授——甚至还没有维基百科页面，这凸显了女性在科学中的持续隐形。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (2018) for chirped pulse amplification
Third woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Technology enables millions of eye surgeries annually
achievementsCn: 因啁啾脉冲放大获诺贝尔物理学奖（2018年）
第三位获诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
技术每年支持数百万次眼科手术
quote: We need to celebrate women physicists because they're out there.
quoteCn: 我们需要赞美女性物理学家，因为她们就在那里。

name: Jocelyn Bell Burnell
bio: As a graduate student in 1967, Jocelyn Bell Burnell discovered pulsars — rapidly rotating neutron stars that emit regular pulses of radio waves. The 1974 Nobel Prize for this discovery was controversially awarded to her supervisor Antony Hewish and Martin Ryle, excluding Bell Burnell. Despite this injustice, she has had an extraordinary career in astrophysics and donated the entire £2.3 million from her 2018 Breakthrough Prize to fund scholarships for underrepresented physics students.
bioCn: 1967年，作为研究生的乔斯林·贝尔·伯奈尔发现了脉冲星——快速旋转的中子星，发射规则的射电脉冲。1974年诺贝尔奖因这一发现颁发给了她的导师安东尼·休伊什和马丁·赖尔，争议性地将贝尔·伯奈尔排除在外。尽管遭受了这种不公正，她在天体物理学方面取得了非凡的职业成就，并将2018年突破奖的全部230万英镑捐赠用于资助代表性不足的物理学生奖学金。
achievements: Discovered pulsars as a graduate student (1967)
Donated £2.3M Breakthrough Prize to student scholarships
Served as president of the Royal Astronomical Society
achievementsCn: 作为研究生发现脉冲星（1967年）
将230万英镑突破奖全部捐赠给学生奖学金
担任皇家天文学会主席
quote: I feel I've done very well out of not getting a Nobel Prize.
quoteCn: 我觉得没获得诺贝尔奖让我的人生过得很好。
references: Burnell, J.B. 'Little Green Men, White Dwarfs, or Pulsars?' Cosmic Search, 1977.

name: Vera Rubin
tagline: Proved the Existence of Dark Matter
bio: Vera Rubin's observations of galaxy rotation curves provided the first strong evidence that most of the matter in the universe is invisible dark matter. Her meticulous work in the 1970s showed that galaxies rotate far faster than expected, implying the existence of vast amounts of unseen mass. Despite revolutionizing our understanding of the cosmos, she was never awarded the Nobel Prize. In 2020, the Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile was named in her honor.
bioCn: 维拉·鲁宾对星系旋转曲线的观测提供了第一个有力证据，表明宇宙中的大部分物质是不可见的暗物质。她在20世纪70年代的细致工作表明，星系的旋转速度远超预期，暗示存在大量看不见的质量。尽管她彻底改变了我们对宇宙的理解，却从未获得诺贝尔奖。2020年，智利的维拉·C·鲁宾天文台以她的名字命名。
achievements: Provided first strong evidence for dark matter
Vera C. Rubin Observatory named in her honor
Received National Medal of Science
achievementsCn: 提供了暗物质的第一个有力证据
维拉·C·鲁宾天文台以她命名
获得国家科学奖章
quote: Science progresses best when observations force us to alter our preconceptions.
quoteCn: 当观测迫使我们改变先入之见时，科学进步最大。
references: Rubin, Vera. Bright Galaxies, Dark Matters. AIP Press, 1997.

name: Cecilia Payne-Gaposchkin
nameCn: 塞西莉亚·佩恩-加波施金
tagline: Discovered What Stars Are Made Of
bio: Cecilia Payne-Gaposchkin's 1925 doctoral thesis was described by astronomer Otto Struve as 'the most brilliant PhD thesis ever written in astronomy.' She demonstrated that stars are composed primarily of hydrogen and helium, contradicting the prevailing belief that they had the same composition as Earth. Initially dissuaded from publishing this conclusion by Henry Norris Russell, her findings were later confirmed and are now fundamental to astrophysics.
bioCn: 塞西莉亚·佩恩-加波施金1925年的博士论文被天文学家奥托·斯特鲁维描述为'天文学史上写得最出色的博士论文'。她证明恒星主要由氢和氦组成，推翻了当时认为恒星与地球成分相同的主流观点。最初她被亨利·诺里斯·罗素劝阻不要发表这一结论，但她的发现后来得到证实，现在已成为天体物理学的基础。
achievements: Discovered stars are primarily hydrogen and helium
Wrote what is called the most brilliant astronomy PhD thesis
First woman to become a full professor at Harvard
achievementsCn: 发现恒星主要由氢和氦组成
撰写了被称为最出色的天文学博士论文
哈佛大学第一位女性正教授
quote: The reward of the young scientist is the emotional thrill of being the first person in the history of the world to see something or to understand something.
quoteCn: 年轻科学家的回报，是成为世界历史上第一个看到或理解某事的人所带来的情感激动。
references: Payne-Gaposchkin, C. An Autobiography and Other Recollections. Cambridge Univ Press, 1984.

name: Hertha Ayrton
taglineCn: 物理学家与电气工程师
bio: Hertha Ayrton was the first woman to read a paper before the Royal Society of London and the first woman to win the Hughes Medal for original scientific research. Her work on electric arcs and ripple marks in sand and water had both theoretical and practical significance. She held 26 patents and was a passionate suffragette. When asked whether she minded being called a woman scientist, she replied that she did not understand the distinction.
bioCn: 赫莎·艾尔顿是第一位在伦敦皇家学会宣读论文的女性，也是第一位因原创科学研究获得休斯奖章的女性。她在电弧以及沙和水中的涟漪方面的工作具有理论和实践双重意义。她拥有26项专利，并且是一位热情的女性参政权运动者。当被问及是否介意被称为女科学家时，她回答说她不理解这种区分。
achievements: First woman to read a paper before the Royal Society
First woman to win the Hughes Medal
Held 26 patents
achievementsCn: 第一位在皇家学会宣读论文的女性
第一位获得休斯奖章的女性
拥有26项专利
references: Sharp, Evelyn. Hertha Ayrton: A Memoir. Edward Arnold, 1926.

name: Andrea Ghez
tagline: Nobel Laureate for Supermassive Black Hole Discovery
taglineCn: 因超大质量黑洞发现获诺贝尔奖
bio: Andrea Ghez shared the 2020 Nobel Prize in Physics for her discovery of a supermassive compact object at the center of the Milky Way galaxy. Using the Keck Observatory in Hawaii, she developed techniques to peer through interstellar dust and tracked stars orbiting the invisible object now known as Sagittarius A*. She became the fourth woman to win the Nobel Prize in Physics.
bioCn: 安德里亚·盖兹因发现银河系中心的超大质量致密天体而分享了2020年诺贝尔物理学奖。利用夏威夷的凯克天文台，她开发了穿透星际尘埃的技术，追踪了围绕现在称为人马座A*的不可见天体运行的恒星。她成为第四位获得诺贝尔物理学奖的女性。
achievements: Nobel Prize in Physics (2020) for black hole discovery
Fourth woman to win Nobel Prize in Physics
Pioneered adaptive optics techniques in astronomy
achievementsCn: 因黑洞发现获诺贝尔物理学奖（2020年）
第四位获诺贝尔物理学奖的女性
开创天文学自适应光学技术

name: Mildred Dresselhaus
nameCn: 米尔德里德·德雷塞尔豪斯
bio: Mildred Dresselhaus, known as the 'Queen of Carbon Science,' was an MIT physicist whose research on carbon nanostructures laid the groundwork for the field of nanotechnology. Her work on graphite, carbon nanotubes, and thermoelectric materials helped make possible technologies from lightweight composites to energy-harvesting devices. She received the Presidential Medal of Freedom and was the first woman to win the National Medal of Science in Engineering.
bioCn: 米尔德里德·德雷塞尔豪斯被称为'碳科学女王'，是MIT物理学家，她对碳纳米结构的研究为纳米技术领域奠定了基础。她在石墨、碳纳米管和热电材料方面的工作使从轻质复合材料到能量收集设备的技术成为可能。她获得了总统自由勋章，是第一位获得国家工程科学奖章的女性。
achievements: Pioneered carbon nanostructure research
Presidential Medal of Freedom recipient
First woman to win National Medal of Science in Engineering
achievementsCn: 开创碳纳米结构研究
获得总统自由勋章
第一位获得国家工程科学奖章的女性
